title: CRISPR-Cas9 episome conjugation into Phaeodactylum tricornutum
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bcv9n","text":"Bacterial-conjugation methodology used to introduce the CRISPR-Cas9 episome generated using the GG2 assembly protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c963i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4do17","text":"This method, though, can be adapted for any episomal transfromation from an E. coli culture that contains a cargo plasmid (episome) and conjugation plasmid (pta-MOB) to Phaeodactylum. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"70pog","text":"Take it easy. Conjugation should be easy and conducted without extremely sensitive steps and difficult techniques. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare conjugation plate Conjugation transformation 6-well culture plate with ASW (0.8% agarose, 5% LB)
Autoclave 45 mL of 1.6% agarose solution (in MQ-H 2 O) Incubate autoclaved bottle/flask in 50 °C waterbath

Pipette 50 mL mL of N-free ASW (Artificial Sea Water) into 250 mL flask Add 5 mL LB media Add 25 µL NaNO 3 (880uM) Ad 25 µL NH 4 Cl (880uM) Incubate ASW+LB in 50 °C waterbath 00:30:00 (or until agarose has acclimated to 50 o C)
Once both solutions have reached 50 °C , pour the agarose into the ASW flask in the PCR hood to maintain sterility Continuually mix in flask until mixed thoroughly
Pipette 8 mL of ASW (0.8% agarose, 5% LB) mix into each well Avoid bubbles on plate by pipetting up 10 mL while only dispensing 8 mL
During incubation, place 2 6-well culture plates (flat bottom) in a PCR hood
Solidify and dry plates in PCR hood 00:30:00 During, prepare P. tricornutum cells
Obtain an axenic culture of Phaeodactylum tricornutum (CCAP1055/1) growing in exponential phase
Determine culture health by Fv/Fm measurement using Dual-PAM fluorometer (Exponentially growing culture typically has a Fv/Fm value of 0.6 to 0.7)

Collect enough culture volume for 6e8 cells (total) Calculation: 100 µL P. tricornutum at concentration of 5e8 cells/mL per well 12 wells X 100 µL = 1.2 mL (5e8 cells/mL) --> 6e8 cells Ex: P.t. concentration = 1.1e6 cells/mL 6e8 (cells) / 1.1e6 (cells/mL) = 545.5 mL *** I round up to 600 mL ***
Measure cell concentration (Ideally by flow cytometry side scatter (SSC) vs. FL3 (longpass filter, 570nm)
Pellet cells 3000 xg 00:15:00
Remove supernatent with graduated pipette-man so to not disturb pellet Add cells to 2 2mL tubes
Pellet cells again 2000 xg 00:05:00 After second pelleting
Remove supernant with pipette
Add 100-200 uL NO 3 -ASW to one 2 mLtube and mix cells with pipette Mix all cells together in 1 tube All cells mixed in one tube. Line drawn on tube indicates 1.2 mL position
Add appropriate volume of NO 3 -ASW to cells to bring final volume to 1.2 mL Mixed cells in final volume of 1.2 mL
Prepare to plat cells onto drying 6-well ASW (0.8% agarose, 5% LB)
Pipette 100 µL P. tricornutum cells on each well, directly in the middle of the well
Spread cells by rotating plate in an orbital motion with hands

Dry cells after spreading under PCR hood 00:20:00 Wet cells on plates in need of drying! Dried cells (evident by loss of sheen on culture surface) Successfully prepare conjugation transformation plate with dried P.t. cells.
Cover 6-well plates and seal lid to plate with breath-easy culture tape
Incubate cells at 18 °C and under medium-intensity light 50 uE m-2 ) 24:00:00
The night before, grow E. coli cultures in preparation for conjugation
Inocculate 2 mL LB media cultures with pipette stab of conjugation-ready E.coli Ampicillin 100 ug/mL Tetricyclin 10 ug/mL Gentimycin 20 ug/mL 37 °C 16:00:00 shaking

Ensure to include positive and negative controls Positive (conjugation episome containing a RFP bacterial expression cassette) Negative (conjugation episome that confers resistance to nourseothricin antibiotic, in contrast to resistance to phleomycin)
Next day, remove E. coli cultures from 37 °C 10 conjugation E.coli cultures. The positive control can be seen with a red hue, indicative of stable episomal maintenance in E. coli
Prepare out-growth E. coli culture media Prepare 12.5 mL LB media (Amp-50, Tet-5, Gent-20) for each conjugation and pipette into 50 mL conical tube (one per conjugation reaction)
Pipette 250 µL overnight E.coli culture to each 50 mL out-growth tube Do not seal tube with cap. Rather, place cap on tube loosely and secure with tape
Shake out-growth cultures a 37 °C 05:00:00
Measure optical density of out-growth cultures Ideal absorbance reading: OD 600 = 0.9-1.0
Pellet out-growth cultures 3000 xg 00:15:00
During pelleting, dry 6-well conjugatin plates under a PCR hood and label each well accordingly

After pelleting, remove media using vacuum manifold Pelleted E. coli. Negative control and positive control (RED) shown After removal of media. Avoid disrupting pellet but remove as much liquid as possible
Resuspend E.coli pellet
Pipette 100 µL S.O.C. media into each tube
Mix well by vortexing
Add E. coli to P. tricornutum cells on conjugation plates

For each conjugation reaction, pipette 100 µL E. coli culture on top of P. tricornutum cells in the middle of the well. Mix the E. coli culture very well with a pipette prior to dispensing in well. Spread E. coli in an orbital motion (refer to video) for each well (do not wait until all 6 wells have E. coli to spread, spread after every dispensing)
Dry E. coli on top of P. tricornutum cells (10-20 minutes) One well (bottom right) with wet E. coli dispensed and spread Almost dry well.... Completely dry well with E. coli on top of P. tricornutum
The conjugation mechanistically operates when the E. coli are exposed to an optimal growth temperature and for a certain duration. P. tricornutum is most competent to conjugation at 30 °C 01:30:00
After all E. coli have dried, wrap each plate with breath-easy tape
Incubate 6-well plate at 30 °C 01:30:00 and in the dark
After incubation, place conjugation plates at 18 °C 50 uE m-2 for 48:00:00

After 48 hrs, collect P. tricorntum cells in 2 mL tubes Remove conjugation plate(s) from incubation and put them under a PCR hood, take off lid to start drying plate(s) Remove lids in PCR hood to start drying cells. The Cas9 negatie control (RFP) conjugation still has a red color

Obtain ASW media supplemented with both NO 3 and NH 4 Prepare 2mL tubes to collect scraped cells I recommend using a hockey stick styled cell scraper. It allows more control of scraping in a small well area Pictured are all materials required for cell scraping: P1000, filtered tips, NH4 ASW media, 2mL tubes (1 for each conjugation) and conjugation plate with cells ready for scraping
For each well... Pipette 500 µL media and gently scrape off cells with a hockey-stick shaped cell spreader *** DO NOT USE A LOOP *** Collect cells with a P1000 pipette Repeat once

Mix collected cells very well with pipette Place tubes at 18 °C 50 uE m-2 for 01:00:00 2mL tubes with scraped cells are incubated at 18C, under irradiant white light (50 uE/m2) and shaking on an orbital shaker. This allows any adherent cells that formed chunks to seperate and homogenize prior to selection
During 1 hr incubation, dry P. tricornutum selection plates

Selection Plates: For each conjugation reaction, prepare 3 selection plates (100mm x 15mm culture plates) with 35 mL media each Ex: Prepare 650 mL of plate selection media (enough for 1 6-well conjugation plate, 18 plates) 1. Autoclave 325 mL 1.6% agarose 2. Incubate at 50 °C after sterilization 3. Incubate 325 mL ASW supplemented with 163 µL NaNO 3 and 163 µL NH 4 Cl, also at 50 °C 4. Thaw Phleomycin stock (100mg/mL) and chloramphenicol (20 mg/mL) 5. Pour agarose into ASW flask and mix 6. Add 325 µL Phleomycin (50 ug/mL final concentration) and 325 µL Chloramphenicol (10 ug/mL final concentration) when temperature of ASW/agarose falls below 50 °C 7. Pipette 35 mL selection media into each culture plate 8. Dry under PCR hood for 00:20:00 9. Store at 4 °C until needed
Dry selection plates under a PCR hood for at least 00:20:00
Pipette 300 uL of collected P. tricornutum cells per plates cells on selection plate (300uL, 300uL, ~200uL (remaining volume ))
Spread on plate with cell spreader (or loop is fine here) cells spread on selection plate

Dry cells on selection plates and seal plates with breath-easy tape cells ought to completely dry on selection before sealing. Pictured are two plates. I use reflection of near-by lighting to determine dryness. The left plate is almost dry with patches of wet (lower right corner of plate) and the right plate is completely dry (less reflective than wet area)
Incubate selected P. tricornutum at 18 °C 50 uE m-2 for at least 10 days or until dark circular colonies begin forming.
creator: Mark Moosburner
